**Monday, July 21, 2025**

**ANNOUNCEMENT:**

Following the Mayor’s directive to suspend classes due to the Heavy Rainfall Warning, all student applicants scheduled for testing on July 21 at 9:00 AM will be RESCHEDULED.

Please stay safe and dry, everyone!

# Temporary Easing of School Uniform Policy

**Tuesday, April 7, 2026**

𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓

In anticipation of 𝐞𝐱𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞 𝐡𝐞𝐚𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 in the coming days, a 𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐩𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐝.

Effective 𝐀𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐥 𝟏𝟑, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔 𝐨𝐧𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐬, students are allowed to wear civilian attire from Monday to Thursday upon entering the school premises, provided that they strictly adhere to the prescribed dress code.

Students are reminded to dress appropriately at all times.

---
label: announcement temporary-easing-of-school-uniform-policy

# Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs

**Friday, April 17, 2026**

**Safety Reminder Regarding Cellphone Use on Stairways**  
To ensure the safety of all students and faculty, we are reminding everyone of the following guideline regarding electronic device usage on stairs:

**Policy:**  
Cellphone use is strictly prohibited while ascending or descending staircases. This includes texting, browsing social media, making calls, or playing games.

**Rationale:**  
Stairways are high-traffic areas that require full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly reduces spatial awareness and reac
label: guideline avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs/]
ire full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly reduces spatial awareness and reaction time, increasing the risk of:

* Slips, trips and falls
* Collisions with other students
* Blocking the flow of traffic during passing periods

**Procedure:**

* **Pause:** If you must send a message or answer a call, step to the side and stop walking before using your device.
* **Pocket:** Keep phones stored in pockets or bags while navigating steps.
* **Proceed:** Only resume walking once the device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.

Thank you for helping us keep our c
label: guideline avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs/]
he device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.

Thank you for helping us keep our campus safe.

---

# GC 27th Founding Anniversary Schedule

**Monday, February 23, 2026**

Gordon College proudly invites the entire GC community to take part in the 27th Founding Anniversary Celebration happening from February 24 to February 26.

Get ready for three days filled with meaningful activities, campus spirit, and memorable moments as we come together to celebrate our legacy, achievements, and the vibrant community that makes GC truly exceptional.

Mark your calendars and join us as we celebrate this milestone in Gordon College history.

See you, GCians!

# Implementation of the School Uniform Policy

**Wednesday, July 30, 2025**

𝐀𝐃𝐕𝐈𝐒𝐎𝐑𝐘

The implementation of the school uniform policy will begin on 𝐒𝐞𝐩𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟖, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐅𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐡𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐬, while 𝐒𝐨𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐒𝐞𝐧𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐨 𝐰𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐬 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐀𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝟏𝟖, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓.

𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 must accomplish their Uniform Exemption Pass from 𝐀𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝟏 𝐭𝐨 𝟏𝟓, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓 𝐚𝐭 𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐦 𝟏𝟐𝟐 at the office of our Student Discipline Officers.

The following shall 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐛𝐞 𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐝:

1. Sleeveless tops, spaghetti straps, razor backs, tubes, hanging or mid rib blouse
2. All types of shorts (walk
label: guideline implementation-of-the-school-uniform-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/implementation-of-the-school-uniform-policy/]
s tops, spaghetti straps, razor backs, tubes, hanging or mid rib blouse
2. All types of shorts (walking shorts, cycling shorts, tokong) tight fitting (leggings) and torn or mutilated jeans
3. Clothes with frayed hems
4. Blouses and dresses with plunging necklines/backless
5. Garments that are backless and See-through attires
6. Miniskirts (more than 2 inches above the knee) except where curriculum requires.
7. Long Skirts with high slit
8. Jogging pants (allowed only for PE schedule)
9. Slippers (exemptions: medical reason, weather conditions)
10. T-shirts with vulgar, indecent, suggestive poi
label: guideline implementation-of-the-school-uniform-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/implementation-of-the-school-uniform-policy/]
 (exemptions: medical reason, weather conditions)
10. T-shirts with vulgar, indecent, suggestive points, or anything which advertise or promote the use of drugs, alcohol, or tobacco.
11. Clogs (e.g., Crocs) may be worn if for medical reasons and during inclement weather (heavy rains and excessive heat).

# Suspension of Transactions

**Thursday, December 11, 2025**

ANNOUNCEMENT  
December 11, 2025

**Suspension of Transactions**

Please be informed that all College transactions will be suspended from Monday, December 15, to Tuesday, December 16, 2025, due to the Strategic Planning for all College Personnel.

Additionally, transactions at the College Registrar’s Office will be suspended earlier, on Friday, December 12, 2025, for their attendance to Assembly of Registrars in Region 3.

All transactions will resume on Wednesday, December 17, 2025.

Thank you for your understanding.

---

# Clean As You Go & Bring Your Own Trash Policy

**Friday, August 8, 2025**

Instructions for Gordon College Students: Clean As You Go & Bring Your Own Trash Policy

Purpose:

To foster a clean and respectful campus environment while promoting sustainability through personal responsibility for waste.

—

Before Class or Activity:

1. Prepare Your Materials:

– Bring Your Own Trash: Carry a small, reusable bag or container to collect any waste you generate during class or activities. This could be a sturdy tote, a lunch bag, or any suitable container.

– Plan for Snacks/Drinks: If you plan to c
label: guideline clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy/]
e a sturdy tote, a lunch bag, or any suitable container.

– Plan for Snacks/Drinks: If you plan to consume food or beverages, pack them in a way that minimizes waste (e.g., reusable containers, bottles).

—

During Class or Activity:

2. Maintain Your Space:

– Keep Your Area Clean: Throughout the session, stay vigilant about your surroundings. Avoid leaving papers, wrappers, or any personal items strewn about.

– Immediate Clean-Up: If you create a mess (accidental spills, paper cuts, etc.), address it promptly. If cleaning supplies are available, use them to tidy up immediately.

3. Responsi
label: guideline clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy/]
dress it promptly. If cleaning supplies are available, use them to tidy up immediately.

3. Responsible Waste Management:

– Use Your Reusable Container: Whenever you have waste, place it in your reusable bag or container. Keep it nearby for easy access.

– Respect Shared Spaces: Be mindful of not just your space but also those around you. Dispose of any common area waste (e.g., napkins from community snacks) in your own trash.

—

After Class or Activity:

4. Final Cleanup:

– Inspect Your Area:

Before leaving, take a moment to ensure your seating area and any communal spaces (tables, chairs
label: guideline clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy/]
:

Before leaving, take a moment to ensure your seating area and any communal spaces (tables, chairs) are tidy.

– Empty Your Trash into Appropriate Bins: When you leave the classroom or activity, empty your reusable container at designated trash or recycling bins outside the classroom (if available). Take anything that belongs to you with you.

5. Dispose Responsibly:

– Recycle When Possible: If your waste is recyclable, place it in the recycling bin. Be sure to check the college’s recycling guidelines to ensure proper disposal.

—

Additional Responsibilities:

6. Encourage a Culture of Cle
label: guideline clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy/]
uidelines to ensure proper disposal.

—

Additional Responsibilities:

6. Encourage a Culture of Cleanliness:

– Remind Peers:If you notice a classmate leaving a mess, gently remind them or offer to help.

– Lead by Example: Demonstrating your commitment to the “Clean As You Go” policy can inspire your peers to do the same.

7. Feedback and Improvement:

– Participate in discussions or feedback sessions organized by the college to share your insights on how the Clean As You Go policy is working.

8. Engage with Initiatives:

– Stay updated on any college-led environmental initiatives or clean-
label: guideline clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy/]


8. Engage with Initiatives:

– Stay updated on any college-led environmental initiatives or clean-up days, and participate actively when you can.

—

By adhering to these instructions, you contribute to creating a cleaner, more sustainable campus environment while developing valuable habits of personal responsibility and community stewardship. Thank you for doing your part!

# DISINFECTION NOTICE

**Friday, October 17, 2025**

October 17, 2025

Due to the recent increase in influenza cases, the Olongapo City Health Office will conduct disinfection procedures at the Gordon College Main and Annex Buildings today, October 17, 2025, at 1:00 p.m.

Thus, afternoon work and face-to-face transactions in the buildings are suspended; classes will shift to online delivery, with all face-to-face activities scheduled to resume tomorrow, October 18, 2025.

# CCTV Privacy and Consent Notices

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [LIMC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/limc/)

**Monday, January 2, 2023**

To all stakeholders of Gordon College, please be informed that the Closed-Circuit Television (CCTV) cameras installed in the main building and premises of the college are used to process personal information, as defined by Republic Act 10173 or the Data Privacy act of 2012. These cameras capture and record the identities of individuals within their scope and are primarily intended for security and safety purposes. As mandated
label: announcement cctv-privacy-and-consent-notices

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/cctv-privacy-and-consent-notices/]
viduals within their scope and are primarily intended for security and safety purposes. As mandated by the law, we would like to remind you that your consent is required for the processing of your personal information captured by these CCTV cameras. Your continued presence within the college premises is considered an implied consent to the processing of your personal information by the CCTV cameras. Rest assured that all personal information captured by the CCTV cameras will be treated with the utmost confidentiality and will only be accessed by authorized personnel for legitimate purposes. Th
label: announcement cctv-privacy-and-consent-notices

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/cctv-privacy-and-consent-notices/]
utmost confidentiality and will only be accessed by authorized personnel for legitimate purposes. Thank you for your cooperation in ensuring compliance with the Data Privacy act of 2012.

# EASE ON SCHOOL UNIFORMPOLICY DUE TO EXTREMEHEAT CONDITIONS (EXTENSION)

[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/)

**Saturday, May 4, 2024**

𝙀𝙮𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚, 𝙂𝘾𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙨!

Due to the expected hot and humid weather conditions, the relaxation of the uniform policy will continue to be in effect throughout the month of May.

𝙁𝙧𝙤𝙢 𝙈𝙤𝙣𝙙𝙖𝙮𝙨 𝙩𝙤 𝙏𝙝𝙪𝙧𝙨𝙙𝙖𝙮𝙨, students will have the option to wear civilian attire, 𝙬𝙝𝙞𝙡𝙚 𝙨𝙩𝙞𝙡𝙡 𝙛𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙤𝙬𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙙𝙧𝙚𝙨𝙨 𝙘𝙤𝙙𝙚 𝙥𝙤𝙡𝙞𝙘𝙮.

Refer to <https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/2050/> for more information.

# Online Application for GC Admission Test Procedure (1st Semester, A.Y. 2024-2025)

[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/)

**Monday, December 18, 2023**

**STEP 1. CREATE ONLINE PORTAL ACCOUNT**

1.1 Go to Gordon College Admission Portal login page (<https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gca>)

1.2 Click “Create Account”

1.3 Type your email address, given name, last name, date of birth, contact number, desired password and select type of student (New Student, Transferee, Second Courser)

1.4 Click “Register”

Notes:

o A valid and existing email address is required in this process.

o
label: guideline online-application-for-gc-admission-test-procedure-1st-semester-a-y-2024-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/online-application-for-gc-admission-test-procedure-1st-semester-a-y-2024-2025/]

1.4 Click “Register”

Notes:

o A valid and existing email address is required in this process.

o Do not create multiple accounts as this will be a ground for disapproval of application.

· Do not forget your email and password

**STEP 2. LOG ON TO YOUR ACCOUNT**

2.1 Go to the portal login page

2.2 Type your previously registered email address and password then click “Log In”

2.3 You will be directed to the portal Dashboard.

2.4 The Dashboard displays the status of your application.

**STEP 3. PROFILING**

3.1 On the upper left side of the page, click “Upload Photo” to upload your recent
label: guideline online-application-for-gc-admission-test-procedure-1st-semester-a-y-2024-2025

P 3. PROFILING**

3.1 On the upper left side of the page, click “Upload Photo” to upload your recent photo.

Guide:

o 2×2 colored picture, white background

o Formal pose wearing collared top, No eyeglasses or any accessories like earrings.

o Picture should be taken not later than one week prior to filing of application.

3.2 On the Menu, click “Profile”

3.3 Completely and accurately fill out the forms (Personal Information, Family Background and Desired Programs)

3.4 Read the Gordon College General Data Privacy Notice and click the CHECKBOX.

3.5 Review the form and click “Submit”

**STEP
label: guideline online-application-for-gc-admission-test-procedure-1st-semester-a-y-2024-2025

 General Data Privacy Notice and click the CHECKBOX.

3.5 Review the form and click “Submit”

**STEP 4. UPLOADING OF DOCUMENTS**

4.1 On the Menu, click “Uploads”

4.2 Upload scanned or clear image of the required documents. Click the placeholder to browse for an image to upload.

4.3 The image file should be less than 2MB to avoid uploading errors

Required documents:

o Recent Good Moral Character Certificate

o PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate

o Senior High School ID or Any Valid ID Card

– Click “Submit” to finalize the uploading.

**STEP 5. GCAT Schedule**

5.1 The schedule of GCAT wi
label: guideline online-application-for-gc-admission-test-procedure-1st-semester-a-y-2024-2025

– Click “Submit” to finalize the uploading.

**STEP 5. GCAT Schedule**

5.1 The schedule of GCAT will be posted to your portal account within three (3) working days after submission of COMPLETE application and required documents.

5.2 Upon posting of the test schedule on the online portal, you may download and print the following:

o GCAT Schedule slip

o Student Information Sheet

What to bring on the test day?

1. GCAT Schedule Slip

2. Student Information Sheets

3. Senior High School or any valid ID Card

5. Pencil and Pen

# THE 4TH GORDON COLLEGE DISTINGUISHED ALUMNI SERVICE AWARD: Call for Nomination 2025

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/)

**Monday, August 4, 2025**

**GENERAL INFORMATION AND GUIDELINES**

Pursuant to Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. (GCAAI) Board Resolution No. 4, series of 2022, the GCAAI establishes the DISTINGUISHED ALUMNI SERVICE AWARD (DASA) to honor Gordon College graduates who have excelled both in their professional and personal careers and who have served as an example to other alumni, students, and the community, both locally and globally. Starting this y
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
 an example to other alumni, students, and the community, both locally and globally. Starting this year, the GCAAI Board of Trustees will recognize the exemplary service and distinguished achievements that brought honor to the Institution and have made significant contributions to society. The GC-DASA recipients will exemplify the core values that the Institution uphold since its establishment: Exceptional Character, Remarkable Excellence, And Unselfish Service.  
  
The GC-DASA shall be the highest honor bestowed by the Alumni Association and will be an event to be celebrated annually as part
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
hest honor bestowed by the Alumni Association and will be an event to be celebrated annually as part of the GC Founding Anniversary and/or GC Alumni Homecoming.  
  
**GENERAL GUIDELINES:**

1. Only graduates of Gordon College (or GC, formerly Olongapo City Colleges) are eligible for the GC-DASA. The nominee shall be a member of the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. as confirmed by its Board.
2. Any GC graduate, faculty member, or stakeholders may make nominations for the GC-DASA. Self–  
   nominations are accepted. The nominee shall accomplish the Official Nomination Form to be provide
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
   nominations are accepted. The nominee shall accomplish the Official Nomination Form to be provided by the GCAAI. No limitation will be placed on the total number of nominations accepted for consideration.
3. The nominee shall have no pending administrative or criminal case upon nomination. He/she shall exhibit a high degree of character & integrity.
4. Only one recipient per college/program will be awarded. In case the college/program will have no  
   nominees/recipients, the awarding ceremony will still commence. Likewise, the GCAAI Board of Trustees can directly, without prejudice to the
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
ny will still commence. Likewise, the GCAAI Board of Trustees can directly, without prejudice to the respective college, nominate and evaluate for such an award though the college/program have already a candidate.
5. Only living candidates are considered. In the event of the death of a candidate between notification of the award and the award ceremony, the award may be given posthumously with the permission of the family and the GCAAI. In the event that the awardee declines to accept the award and/or fails to attend the awarding ceremonies, the GCAAI reserves the right to withdraw the award.
6
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
nd/or fails to attend the awarding ceremonies, the GCAAI reserves the right to withdraw the award.
6. A GC graduate sitting as a member of the selection committee or a member of the GCAAI Board of Trustees is not eligible to receive the award. This individual is eligible for nomination after a one-year waiting period from the date he/she steps down from his/her role.
7. Recipients are only eligible for one GC-DASA in a lifetime.
8. The recipients will receive a plaque or certificate of recognition and will be posted on the official FB page of the GCAAI. It will announce the annual recipients t
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
on and will be posted on the official FB page of the GCAAI. It will announce the annual recipients through various methods and honor each recipient with the appropriate recognition.
9. If for any reason, the GCAAI is not able to conduct the Awards as planned (including due to tampering, unauthorized intervention, fraud, technical difficulties, pandemic, or any other causes beyond the control of the organizer which impact the administration, security, fairness, integrity or proper conduct of the Awards), then they reserve the right in their sole discretion to make any reasonable variation to th
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
Awards), then they reserve the right in their sole discretion to make any reasonable variation to the Awarding Ceremony.
10. The Nomination Committee and/or GCAAI Board of Trustees may disqualify any nominee that they  
    reasonably determine has breached any of the terms and conditions or otherwise engaged in unlawful or other improper conduct that may bring the Awards into disrepute or jeopardize the fair and proper conduct of the Awards.
11. Other guidelines may be promulgated as decided by the GCAAI Board of Trustees.

**NOMINATION COMMITTEE:**  
The Nomination Committee (NC) may be orga
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
 the GCAAI Board of Trustees.

**NOMINATION COMMITTEE:**  
The Nomination Committee (NC) may be organized by the Dean/OIC in every college and/or the GCAAI. The committee must be composed of at least three (3) members from their respective colleges. The total count of committee members should be an odd number. The chairperson of the NC shall be the Dean or its duly authorized representative and for the GCAAI, any member can initiate such nomination. No member of the NC must be related within the second degree of consanguinity or affinity to any of the nominees/candidates for awards. If the Dea
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
 second degree of consanguinity or affinity to any of the nominees/candidates for awards. If the Dean/OIC is related within the second degree of consanguinity or affinity to any of the candidates for awards, the Dean/OIC must inhibit him/herself from participating in the process. The approval shall come from the person next in rank.  
The NC shall:

1. Abide by the criteria set forth by the GCAAI.
2. For nominees endorsed by the College, it shall issue a certificate of authenticity to all the documents that will be submitted to the GCAAI.
3. Deliberate on the qualifications of the nominees for
label: announcement the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/the-4th-gordon-college-distinguished-alumni-service-award-call-for-nomination-2025/]
cuments that will be submitted to the GCAAI.
3. Deliberate on the qualifications of the nominees for the awards based on the criteria and the documents submitted.

# 3rd Distinguished Alumni Service Award: Call for Nomination 2024

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/)

**Wednesday, January 24, 2024**

## THE 3RD GORDON COLLEGE DISTINGUISHED ALUMNI SERVICE AWARD (GC-DASA) FOR THE YEAR 2024

**GENERAL INFORMATION AND GUIDELINES**

Pursuant to Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. (GCAAI) Board Resolution No. 4, series of 2022, the GCAAI establishes the **DISTINGUISHED ALUMNI SERVICE AWARD (DASA)** to honor Gordon College graduates who have excelled both in their professional and personal careers and who have served as an example to other
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
 excelled both in their professional and personal careers and who have served as an example to other alumni, students, and the community, both locally and globally. Starting this year, the GCAAI Board of Trustees will recognize the exemplary service and distinguished achievements that brought honor to the Institution and have made significant contributions to society. The GC-DASA recipients will exemplify the core values that the Institution uphold since its establishment: **Exceptional Character, Remarkable Excellence, And Unselfish Service.**

The GC-DASA shall be the highest honor bestowed
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
r, Remarkable Excellence, And Unselfish Service.**

The GC-DASA shall be the highest honor bestowed by the Alumni Association and will be an event to be celebrated annually as part of the GC Founding Anniversary and/or GC Alumni Homecoming.

## GENERAL GUIDELINES:

1. Only graduates of Gordon College (or GC, formerly Olongapo City Colleges) are eligible for the GC-DASA. The nominee shall be a member of the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. as confirmed by its Board.
2. Any GC graduate, faculty member, or stakeholders may make nominations for the GC-DASA. Self–nominations are accepted. Th
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
ulty member, or stakeholders may make nominations for the GC-DASA. Self–nominations are accepted. The nominee shall accomplish the Official Nomination Form to be provided by the GCAAI. No limitation will be placed on the total number of nominations accepted for consideration.
3. The nominee shall have no pending administrative or criminal case upon nomination. He/she shall exhibit a high degree of character & integrity.
4. Only one recipient per college/program will be awarded. In case the college/program will have no nominees/recipients, the awarding ceremony will still commence. Likewise, th
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
ge/program will have no nominees/recipients, the awarding ceremony will still commence. Likewise, the GCAAI Board of Trustees can directly, without prejudice to the respective college, nominate and evaluate for such an award though the college/program have already a candidate.
5. Only living candidates are considered. In the event of the death of a candidate between notification of the award and the award ceremony, the award may be given posthumously with the permission of the family and the GCAAI. In the event that the awardee declines to accept the award and/or fails to attend the awarding c
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
AI. In the event that the awardee declines to accept the award and/or fails to attend the awarding ceremonies, the GCAAI reserves the right to withdraw the award.
6. A GC graduate sitting as a member of the selection committee or a member of the GCAAI Board of Trustees is not eligible to receive the award. This individual is eligible for nomination after a one-year waiting period from the date he/she step down from his/her role.
7. Recipients are only eligible for one GC-DASA in a lifetime.
8. The recipients will receive a plaque or certificate of recognition and will be posted on the official
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/3rd-dasa-call-nomination/]
he recipients will receive a plaque or certificate of recognition and will be posted on the official FB page of the GCAAI. It will announce the annual recipients through various methods and honor each recipient with the appropriate recognition.
9. If for any reason, the GCAAI is not able to conduct the Awards as planned (including due to tampering, unauthorized intervention, fraud, technical difficulties, pandemic, or any other causes beyond the control of the organizer which impact the administration, security, fairness, integrity or proper conduct of the Awards), then they reserve the right
label: announcement 3rd-dasa-call-nomination

ration, security, fairness, integrity or proper conduct of the Awards), then they reserve the right in their sole discretion to make any reasonable variation to the Awarding Ceremony.
10. The Nomination Committee and/or GCAAI Board of Trustees may disqualify any nominee that they reasonably determine has breached any of the terms and conditions or otherwise engaged in unlawful or other improper conduct that may bring the Awards into disrepute or jeopardize the fair and proper conduct of the Awards.
11. Other guidelines may be promulgated as decided by the GCAAI Board of Trustees.

The Nomination Committee (NC) may be organized by the Dean/OIC in every college and/or the GCAAI. The committee must be composed of at least three (3) members from their respective colleges. The total count of committee members should be an odd number. The chairperson of the NC shall be the Dean or its duly authorized representative and for the GCAAI, any member can initiate such nomination. No member of the NC must be related within the second degree of consanguinity or affinity t

ination. No member of the NC must be related within the second degree of consanguinity or affinity to any of the nominees/candidates for awards. If the Dean/OIC is related within the second degree of consanguinity or affinity to any of the candidates for awards, the Dean/OIC must inhibit him/herself from participating in the process. The approval shall come from the person next in rank.

Log-on to the student portal ([https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student](https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZWpzSlY3MHlQdVJWaXA0bnNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR5DCtJJZeE0IeMkAlOWYkAjlJ5HTa_uyuxL5M64lTq3dzJieHkPXJaQ0YalBg_aem_XfOZPmWrrx4p7jcYpDLqDg))

Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.

Step 2

To request for Dropping of classes

DLqDg))

Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.

Step 2

To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.

To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.

Step 3

Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.

Click submit to finalize your request or cancel to clear selected classes.

Step 4

Step 4

Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be verified by the college registrar.

Regularly visit the GCES portal for request updates.

# 𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧

**Thursday, December 11, 2025**

𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓

𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧

To all 𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐞𝐬, please be advised that the claiming of your financial grants will be held at 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞, 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐇𝐚𝐥𝐥 (𝟑𝐫𝐝 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐫) 𝐨𝐧 𝐃𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟏𝟏–𝟏𝟐, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝟕:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌 𝐭𝐨 𝟓:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌.

This payout covers the following:

• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟕.𝟏 – 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟑–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒

• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

• 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐋𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧 𝟏𝟓 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝

𝟎𝟐𝟒

• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

• 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐋𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧 𝟏𝟓 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

𝐑𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐃𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬

Please prepare the following complete documents :

1. 𝐓𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐭𝐨𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬 of your 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞), each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬

2. 𝐅𝐨𝐫 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐬 (𝐈𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐅𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘) — all in 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬:

a. Original 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐋𝐞𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐫 with the grantee’s wet signature.

b. Photocopy of the 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (with signature) of the grantee, each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬.

c. Photocopy of the 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (with signature) of the authorized representative, each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬.

3. 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐆𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐞𝐬 𝐎𝐧𝐥𝐲

1. Please bring 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐭𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 and strictly follow your 𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐞𝐝 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐥𝐞 to avoid crowding.

# GC ALAB 2026: Job Fair

**Thursday, February 19, 2026**

In celebration of the Gordon College 27th Founding Anniversary,

the GC ALAB Job Fair 2026 is here!

Meet hiring companies, explore career options, and get hired on the spot!

February 25, 2026

Ayala Malls – Harbor Point Activity Center

9:00 AM – 3:00 PM

Open to ALL job seekers. Fresh grads, career shifters, and experienced professionals are welcome. Bring your resume, confidence, and your best professional look!

# OFFICIAL STATEMENT FROM GORDON COLLEGE

**Sunday, January 4, 2026**

Gordon College is aware of the report aired by Bilyonaryo News in January 2026 concerning an alleged ₱19 million overpayment related to the implementation of the Free Higher Education (FHE) program specifically the mismatched definition of laboratory rooms.

In connection with the Commission on Audit’s (COA) rejoinder dated March 14, 2025, regarding Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. CHED-CO-2025-003 concerning the Free Higher Education (FHE) reimbursements to Gordon College for Academic Years 2019-2020 to 2023-2024, th
label: announcement official-statement-from-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/official-statement-from-gordon-college/]
igher Education (FHE) reimbursements to Gordon College for Academic Years 2019-2020 to 2023-2024, the institution submitted its clarifications and justification in coordination with the UniFAST Secretariat and Commission on Audit last May 16, 2025.

The institution’s initial inclusion of the Audio-Visual Room (AVR) fees under laboratory fees was made with good intentions and full transparency. This long-standing policy was openly reflected in the institution’s submitted and approved Tuition and Other School Fees (TOSF), which listed the AVR for laboratory fees. However as early as last year, o
label: announcement official-statement-from-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/official-statement-from-gordon-college/]
Other School Fees (TOSF), which listed the AVR for laboratory fees. However as early as last year, once informed that “laboratory fees” is strictly interpreted to relate to specific courses that use specialized equipment, which the previous classification may not have fully met, the College adjusted accordingly and removed the said fees from the list.

The College wishes to clarify that funds received under the Free Higher Education program are meticulously allocated and budgeted which undergoes strict audit by appropriate national government agencies, including the Commission on Higher Educat
label: announcement official-statement-from-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/official-statement-from-gordon-college/]
 strict audit by appropriate national government agencies, including the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) and UniFAST and the Commission on Audit. All collections approved by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) – UniFAST were allocated transparently for the operations and development of the college.

Gordon College is fully cooperating with concerned government agencies and complying with the recommendations of the Commission on Audit. The institution has always upheld transparency, accountability, and the proper stewardship of public funds. We respectfully call on the public to all
label: announcement official-statement-from-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/official-statement-from-gordon-college/]
ccountability, and the proper stewardship of public funds. We respectfully call on the public to allow due process to take its course and to rely on verified information from official sources. Gordon College remains committed to addressing this matter responsibly and will provide updates as soon as official findings are available.

---
label: announcement official-statement-from-gordon-college

# Proper Usage of Glass Boards

**Thursday, May 23, 2024**

GUIDELINES FOR THE USE OF GLASS BOARD INSTALLED IN THE CLASSROOMS

* Use White Board Marker only to write on it
* Always erase marker ink from the surface after each use to prevent stains and ghosting.
* Don’t use dirty eraser or cloth, it will start spread ink residue across the surface of the board.
* Glass board must be cleared of any markings before the next class starts.
* Avoid using tapes or adhesives on the glass surface. These materials may be difficult and cause permanent damage to the glass board.
* Don’t use your hands to
label: guideline proper-usage-of-glass-boards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/proper-usage-of-glass-boards/]
materials may be difficult and cause permanent damage to the glass board.
* Don’t use your hands to wipe a glass board! Your skin carries natural oils that over time leave a build-up of oil residue on the board. This can eventually ruin the surface of the glass board.
* Do not use sharp objects to avoid scratching or damaging the board surface.
* If the glass board is accidentally broken, it must be replaced with the same brand within seven (7) days from the date it was broken. If the item with specific brand and specification is not available, it must be replaced with three (3) timex of the q
label: guideline proper-usage-of-glass-boards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/proper-usage-of-glass-boards/]
specific brand and specification is not available, it must be replaced with three (3) timex of the quantity or in quantity comparable to the price of the item. The brand and specification must be approved first by the concerned personnel before replacement.

---
label: guideline proper-usage-of-glass-boards

# Offline Servers (July 19 to 20, 2025)

**Friday, July 18, 2025**

**ANNOUNCEMENT**

July 18, 2025

OFFLINE SERVERS

Please be informed that the College’s servers will be offline due to a power interruption from 3:00 PM on July 19, 2025, until July 20, 2025.

This means all digital services of the College will be inaccessible during this period.

All digital services will resume on July 21, 2025.

---

# Shift to Online Classes (July 19, 2025)

**Friday, July 18, 2025**

**ANNOUNCEMENT**

July 18, 2025

SHIFT TO ONLINE CLASSES ADVISORY

Due to inclement weather, please be informed that all classes tomorrow, July 19, 2025, will shift to online modality, including Related Learning Experiences (RLE).

Face-to-face classes will resume on Monday, July 21, 2025.

---

In line with the Student Leaders’ Capacity Building and Team Building, the 𝐎𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐕𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐖𝐞𝐥𝐟𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐉𝐮𝐥𝐲 𝟏𝟔 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝟏𝟕.

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/2050/]
# EASE ON SCHOOL UNIFORM POLICY DUE TO EXTREME HEAT CONDITION

**Friday, April 12, 2024**

𝙀𝙮𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚, 𝙂𝘾𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙨!

The Office recognizes that the well-being of our students is of utmost importance. Thus, in anticipation of extreme heat conditions over the next few days, the Office opted to temporarily ease the school uniform policy 𝙛𝙤𝙧 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙞𝙧𝙚𝙩𝙮 𝙤𝙛 𝘼𝙥𝙧𝙞𝙡.

𝙁𝙧𝙤𝙢 𝙈𝙤𝙣𝙙𝙖𝙮𝙨 𝙩𝙤 𝙏𝙝𝙪𝙧𝙨𝙙𝙖𝙮𝙨, students will have the option to wear civilian attire, 𝙬𝙝𝙞𝙡𝙚 𝙨𝙩𝙞𝙡𝙡 𝙛𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙤𝙬𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙙𝙧𝙚𝙨𝙨 𝙘𝙤𝙙𝙚 𝙥𝙤𝙡𝙞𝙘𝙮.

This adjustment aims to alleviate the discomfort caused by high temperatures.

---

# TALAGHAY AT PAGAHOM PARA SA PULANG PUSO

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/)

**Monday, April 24, 2023**

Join us for a powerful event as we come together to raise awareness about HIV/AIDS and promote prevention through PrEP education and on-site testing.

TALAGHAY AT PAGAHOM PARA SA PULANG PUSO is organized by the Gordon College Alumni Association Inc., League of Empowered Advocate for Development (LEAD) Shelter, and the Kagitingan Community Center Extension by P.O.S.H. # F2F Classes Will Continue

**Sunday, March 5, 2023**

As per CHED Memorandum released on March 3, 2023, all Higher Education Institution were given discretion to conduct appropriate flexible learning modalities for the duration of the nationwide transport strike to avoid disruption of classes. However, as it was reported in the news that transport groups in Central Luzon (including Olongapo City, Zambales, and Bataan) pronounced that they will not be joining the said strike and shall continue their operations, please be informed that Gordon College will continue its scheduled face-to-face classes this week (March 6 to 11, 2023).

---

**Data Privacy Notice for ID Processing**  
**Academic Year 2025–2026**

In compliance with data privacy regulations and our commitment to protecting your personal information, this notice explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your data for the purpose of ID processing.

**1. Purpose of Data Collection**

          We collect and process personal data solely for the issuanc
label: announcement id-card-application-new-replacement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/id-card-application-new-replacement/]
 Purpose of Data Collection**

          We collect and process personal data solely for the issuance and management of institutional ID cards for students, faculty, and staff. This ID serves as an official form of identification within the institution and is used for campus access, academic and administrative transactions, and other institutional services.

**2. Personal Data Collected**

The following personal information will be collected for ID processing:

 • Full Name  
 • Course / Program  
 • Date of Birth  
 • Home Address  
 • Emergency Contact Person (Name and Contact Information)
label: announcement id-card-application-new-replacement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/id-card-application-new-replacement/]
  
 • Date of Birth  
 • Home Address  
 • Emergency Contact Person (Name and Contact Information)

**3. Legal Basis for Processing**

          Your data is processed based on your consent and in accordance with the legitimate interests of the institution to maintain secure and effective identification systems for its stakeholders.

**4. Data Storage and Protection**

          All collected data will be stored securely in our official records system, accessible only to authorized personnel.

Physical and digital safeguards are in place to protect your information from unauthorized access, di
label: announcement id-card-application-new-replacement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/id-card-application-new-replacement/]
hysical and digital safeguards are in place to protect your information from unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction.

**5. Data Sharing and Retention**

          Your personal data will not be shared with third parties outside the institution unless required by law or with your explicit consent. Data will be retained only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purpose of ID processing or as required by applicable regulations.

**6. Your Rights**

          You have the right to:

                   • Access your personal data

                   • Request correction of i

                   • Withdraw your consent (where applicable)

                   • Lodge a complaint with the Data Protection Officer or National Privacy Commission

**7. Contact Information**

𝙀𝙮𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚, 𝙂𝘾𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙨!

Join us for the second session of 𝗚𝗔𝗕𝗔𝗬: 𝗔 𝗦𝗲𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗿 𝗦𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝘁𝗵 𝗼𝗻 𝗟𝗲𝗴𝗮𝗹 𝗟𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘆, 𝗦𝗮𝗳𝗲𝘁𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗝𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗲 with the theme: “𝗨𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗟𝗮𝘄, 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝗥𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝗽𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗽𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗯𝗹𝗲 𝗚𝗲𝗻𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻”

# Gordon College Mag-aaral Resource Program

**Friday, August 15, 2025**

𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓 𝐒𝐓𝐔𝐃𝐄𝐍𝐓𝐒!

A college education is a powerful tool for a student’s future—but for many, 𝐟𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐛𝐮𝐫𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐬 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐛𝐞 𝐚 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐧𝐭 𝐨𝐛𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐥𝐞.

The 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞 𝐌𝐚𝐠-𝐚𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐜𝐞 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐆.𝐂.𝐌.𝐑.𝐏.) is our direct and impactful way to nurture students’ potential while staying true to our commitment to academic excellence and social responsibility.

This vital initiative is made possible through the generous sponsorship of 𝐁𝐂𝐃𝐀 𝐃𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐨𝐫 𝐑𝐨𝐥𝐞𝐧 𝐂. 𝐏𝐚𝐮𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐨, whose vision and support will empower our students to overc
label: announcement gordon-college-mag-aaral-resource-program

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/gordon-college-mag-aaral-resource-program/]
rship of 𝐁𝐂𝐃𝐀 𝐃𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐨𝐫 𝐑𝐨𝐥𝐞𝐧 𝐂. 𝐏𝐚𝐮𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐨, whose vision and support will empower our students to overcome financial hurdles and focus on their education.

𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐏𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐝:

𝐀𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝟏𝟖 – 𝟐𝟗, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐚𝐯𝐚𝐢𝐥𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞

Note:

Slots are limited, first-come basis, subject to eligibility, and completeness of requirements.

# Gordon College Takbo Para Sa Guro 2025

**Saturday, October 4, 2025**

𝗔𝗡𝗡𝗢𝗨𝗡𝗖𝗘𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧!

Gordon College Takbo Para Sa Guro 2025

Due to weather conditions, the 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗙𝗮𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘆 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗚𝗖𝗙𝗔𝘀) announces that the 𝗧𝗮𝗸𝗯𝗼 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝗮 𝗦𝗮 𝗚𝘂𝗿𝗼 𝗩𝗜 on 𝗢𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝟱, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 at 𝗢𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴𝗮𝗽𝗼 𝗖𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗦𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝘀 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲𝘅 will be rescheduled to 𝗢𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝟭𝟮, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱.

The safety and well-being of everyone remain our top priority. We look forward to running with you as we honor the dedication, hard work, and invaluable contribution of teachers in shaping lives and communities. See you at the starting line!

For more information,

# Application for Graduation (Batch 2026)

**Wednesday, October 29, 2025**

This is to notify all prospective graduates (Batch 2026) that the schedule of filing of application for graduation is from November 3 to 14, 2025.

Please be guided by the following:

Step 1. Using your GC domain email address, fill-out the Google form completely thru this link. [https://forms.gle/XrhsaZ9jRxcQBfaF8](https://forms.gle/XrhsaZ9jRxcQBfaF8?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExYmtONXprRWlaY05XUDNlanNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR7TEZI8dB-X6KBTyfrV2_znyUZh2Bg4TCEqcWhJ8fWaKvfJir4E7JKmNAupgw_aem_TBRrrONPSK

DIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR7TEZI8dB-X6KBTyfrV2_znyUZh2Bg4TCEqcWhJ8fWaKvfJir4E7JKmNAupgw_aem_TBRrrONPSKL1EnrueOp7RQ)

– Accomplished Prospectus (You may access the Academic Evaluation Sheet and Summary of Grades from the GCES portal for your reference)

Step 3. You will be notified of the status of your application via email as soon as possible.

𝗔𝗗𝗩𝗜𝗦𝗢𝗥𝗬

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/prc-on-site-services-caravan/]
# PRC On-site Services Caravan

**Friday, August 15, 2025**

𝐍𝐨 𝐌𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐋𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬 — 𝐏𝐑𝐂 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐚𝐭 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞!

The 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐟𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐠𝐮𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐢𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 (𝐏𝐑𝐂) is coming to you! The 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞 𝐀𝐥𝐮𝐦𝐧𝐢 𝐀𝐬𝐬𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐈𝐧𝐜. will spearhead a 𝐓𝐰𝐨-𝐃𝐚𝐲 𝐒𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 and 𝐑𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐰𝐚𝐥 𝐨𝐟 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐟𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐋𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐧𝐬𝐞/𝐈𝐃 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐬 on 𝐒𝐞𝐩𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟐𝟓–𝟐𝟔, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, from 𝟖:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌 𝐭𝐨 𝟓:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌 𝐚𝐭 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞, 𝐎𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠𝐚𝐩𝐨 𝐂𝐢𝐭𝐲.

This initiative, in partnership with Renato Cultivo Customs Brokerage, Gordon College – Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) Alumni Affairs Unit, and in collaboration with
label: announcement prc-on-site-services-caravan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/prc-on-site-services-caravan/]
 Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) Alumni Affairs Unit, and in collaboration with PRC Region III, aims to make PRC services more accessible to professionals and aspiring licensees.

𝐀𝐯𝐚𝐢𝐥𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬:

Application for Licensure Examination

Initial Registration of Passers (for those who have attended the oath-taking)

Renewal & Duplication of Professional Identification Card

Issuance of Certifications & Authentication of PRC Documents

Petition for Change of Status/Registered Name due to Marriage/Correction of Entries/Data

𝐕𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞 (Main Building – Ground Floo

# Duyan ng Pag-asa: Tulong para sa Nasalanta

**Thursday, July 24, 2025**

The recent typhoon and intensified habagat have left many barangays in Olongapo City flooded and displaced. In response, the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) and Research Development and Community Extension Services (Gordon College-Rdces) are launching a donation drive to support affected families.

𝙇𝙚𝙩’𝙨 𝙘𝙤𝙢𝙚 𝙩𝙤𝙜𝙚𝙩𝙝𝙚𝙧—𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙙𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙨, 𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙙𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙤𝙧𝙜𝙖𝙣𝙞𝙯𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙨, 𝙛𝙖𝙘𝙪𝙡𝙩𝙮, 𝙖𝙙𝙢𝙞𝙣, 𝙖𝙡𝙪𝙢𝙣𝙞, 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙨𝙩𝙖𝙠𝙚𝙝𝙤𝙡𝙙𝙚𝙧𝙨—𝙩𝙤 𝙪𝙥𝙡𝙞𝙛𝙩 𝙡𝙞𝙫𝙚𝙨 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙘𝙧𝙖𝙙𝙡𝙚 𝙤𝙪𝙧 𝙛𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙤𝙬 𝙊𝙡𝙤𝙣𝙜𝙖𝙥𝙚ñ𝙤𝙨 𝙞𝙣 𝙖 𝙩𝙧𝙪𝙚 𝙨𝙥𝙞𝙧𝙞𝙩 𝙤𝙛 𝙗𝙖𝙮𝙖𝙣𝙞𝙝𝙖𝙣.

“𝗗𝘂𝘆𝗮𝗻 𝗻𝗴 𝗣𝗮𝗴-𝗮𝘀𝗮: 𝗧𝘂𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮 𝘀𝗮 𝗡

“𝗗𝘂𝘆𝗮𝗻 𝗻𝗴 𝗣𝗮𝗴-𝗮𝘀𝗮: 𝗧𝘂𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮 𝘀𝗮 𝗡𝗮𝘀𝗮𝗹𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗮” is our shared effort to extend hope and compassion through in-kind or cash donations.

# Online Application for Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT) for First Semester A.Y. 2023-2024

**Monday, December 12, 2022**

Dec. 12, 2022 – Marc. 31, 2023

Please be informed that all applications shall be submitted online to prevent the spread of Covid-19.

**STEP 1. CREATE ONLINE PORTAL ACCOUNT**

– Go to Gordon College Admission Portal login page (<https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/apps/gca/student/#/login>)

– Click “Create Account”

– Type your email address, given name, last name, date of birth, contact number, desired password and select type of student (New Student, Transferee, Secon

Notes:

o A valid and existing email address is required in this process.

o Do not create multiple accounts as this will be a ground for disapproval of application

o Do not forget your email and password

– Type your previously registered email address and password then click “Log In”

– You will be directed to the portal Dashboard.

– The Dashboard displays the status of your application.

You will not be able to proceed with the enlistment process until you are cleared of all accountabilities.

label: guideline online-enlistment-for-enrolment-procedure-for-midyear-a-y-2022-2023-may-29-to-june-9-2023

# Adding and Dropping of Classes via Student Portal

**Tuesday, January 31, 2023**

College students may add/drop classes for Second Semester Academic Year 2022-2023 from January 31 to February 10, 2023.

Step 1

Log-on to the student portal (<https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/ccs/students/gces/#/login>)

Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.

Step 2

To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.

To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) o
label: guideline adding-and-dropping-of-classes-via-student-portal

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/adding-and-dropping-of-classes-via-student-portal/]
ts section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.

Step 3

Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.

Click submit to finalize your request or cancel to clear selected classes.

Step 4

Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be approved by the college registrar. Regularly visit the portal for updates.

---
label: guideline adding-and-dropping-of-classes-via-student-portal

# Online Enlistment for Enrolment Procedure for Continuing and Re-admitted Students (2nd Semester 2022-2023)

**Friday, December 9, 2022**

**Schedule:** December 19, 2022 – January 6, 2023

**Instructions:**

1. Log-on to the student portal gces.gordoncollege.edu.ph.  
   Note: A “forgot password request” option is available in case of trouble logging in.
2. Update your profile.
3. Upload scanned/clear image of the following enrolment requirement/s -> Recent Certificate of Residency (In case of change of address)
4. Update academic and enlistment information.

6. Completing all the steps above will register/enlist you for enrolment.
7. The status of your enrolment will be posted on the student portal.

**Friday, March 10, 2023**

STEP 1. UPLOAD ACADEMIC RECORDS

After taking the Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT), applicants should upload academic records on the GCA portal for screening and evaluation.

The DEADLINE of uploading is on April 30, 2023.

· Log-on to the GCA portal (<https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/apps/gca/student>).

· On the Menu, click “Uploads”

· Upload scanned or clear image of one of the following documents:

A. Grade 12 students who are expected to graduate at the end of Academic Year 2

# Adding/Dropping of Classes via GC Student Portal

**Tuesday, August 15, 2023**

Adding/Dropping of Classes via GC Student Portal

College students may add/drop classes for First Semester Academic Year 2023-2024 from August 14-25, 2023.

Step 1

Log-on to the student portal (<https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/ccs/students/gces/#/login>)

Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.

Step 2

To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.

To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class
label: guideline adding-dropping-of-classes-via-gc-student-portal

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/adding-dropping-of-classes-via-gc-student-portal/]
dding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.

Step 3

Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.

Click submit to finalize your request or cancel to clear selected classes.

Step 4

Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be approved by the college registrar. Regularly visit the portal for updates.

---
label: guideline adding-dropping-of-classes-via-gc-student-portal

Attention all students and parents, please be informed that GC-Senior High School Department will no longer accept students under CHED Memorandum No. 33 series 2015 and BOT Resolution No. 6 series of 2021.

We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

We encourage you to explore other educational institutions for your academic pursuits.

Thank you for your support and understanding.

# Limited Elevator Access

**Wednesday, February 12, 2025**

This is to inform you that, due to ongoing periodic maintenance, the elevators within our facility will have limited access for the time being.

Effective immediately, the use of elevators will be restricted to the following individuals:

* Senior citizens
* Pregnant women
* Persons with disabilities (PWD)
* Individuals carrying heavy items

This measure is necessary to ensure the safety and integrity of the elevators during this maintenance period. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this matter.

We will keep you up

enance period. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this matter.

Thank you for your attention and understanding.

# Schedule and Guidelines of Annual Medical Examination for A.Y. 2023-2024

**Thursday, May 25, 2023**

As part of the annual requirement for enrollment, students are required to submit laboratory tests to the College Clinic and undergo Medical Examination.

Below is the process in obtaining a Medical Certificate:

1. Present the request form to the medical laboratory of your choice.

𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗲: The request form will be provided by your program coordinator.

2. Once you have the laboratory results, second-year to fourth-year students must book an appointment thru this link: [bit.ly/GCMCUpperClass](
label: announcement schedule-and-guidelines-of-annual-medical-examination-for-a-y-2023-2024

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/schedule-and-guidelines-of-annual-medical-examination-for-a-y-2023-2024/]
econd-year to fourth-year students must book an appointment thru this link: [bit.ly/GCMCUpperClass](http://bit.ly/GCMCUpperClass)

𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗲: for incoming freshmen, the link will be provided by their respective coordinator after their interview.

3. The Health Services Unit will confirm the appointment date thru email. 𝗢𝗻𝗹𝘆 𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 with an appointment will be entertained on the 𝘀𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗱𝘂𝗹𝗲𝗱 𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗲.
4. On the Physical Examination day, bring the following:  
   ✅All the original results of your laboratory tests (an updated result or at least 2 months period);  
   ✅1×1 picture; and  
   ✅vaccine card

# Paglalayag 2023: Navigating towards New Horizons, Sailing Beyond 25 Years

**Friday, August 11, 2023**

𝐖𝐞 𝐤𝐧𝐨𝐰 𝐲𝐨𝐮’𝐫𝐞 𝐞𝐱𝐜𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐝!

On Monday, we are kicking off the Academic Year 2023-2024 with 𝗔𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮 – the tradition of welcoming back students to the campus!

This year’s theme is “𝗣𝗮𝗴𝗹𝗮𝗹𝗮𝘆𝗮𝗴 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟯: 𝗡𝗮𝘃𝗶𝗴𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗼𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗱𝘀 𝗡𝗲𝘄 𝗛𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗼𝗻𝘀, 𝗦𝗮𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗕𝗲𝘆𝗼𝗻𝗱 𝟮𝟱 𝗬𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘀”

Here are your reminders to have a memorable experience of Apertura:

1. Freshmen will line up at East Tapinac Court while the sophomores, juniors, and seniors will line up in the field facing Gordon College Campus.

2. Wear your college col
label: announcement paglalayag-2023-navigating-towards-new-horizons-sailing-beyond-25-years

niors, and seniors will line up in the field facing Gordon College Campus.

2. Wear your college color:

❤️ Red – College of Allied Health Studies

💛Yellow – College of Business and Accountancy

🧡Orange – College of Computer Studies

💙Blue – College of Education, Arts, and Sciences

💖Pink – College of Hospitality and Tourism Management

\*𝘋𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴 𝘊𝘰𝘥𝘦 𝘸𝘪𝘭𝘭 𝘴𝘵𝘪𝘭𝘭 𝘣𝘦 𝘰𝘣𝘴𝘦𝘳𝘷𝘦𝘥.

3. Bring an umbrella, bottled water, and wear a face mask.

4. Be on time and don’t miss the fun!

* Watch performances from the Olongapo City Marching Band, from our very own PASE Voci and PASE Ballo too!
* Fun activities

Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00) educational cash assistance every semester to qualified students.

# Earthquake Drill

**Thursday, June 8, 2023**

To all GC stakeholders:

Please be informed that the Gordon College will be participating to the Nationwide Simultaneous Earthquake Drill 2023. The GC DRRM Team will facilitate the simulation with the following protocols:

1. 1 minute long alarm (duck, cover and hold on)
2. Evacuate and assembly
3. Drill will be declared over after the headcount.

This should take 10-15 minutes from the onset of the alarm.

For your guidance.

---
label: announcement earthquake-drill

# Employment Enhancement Webinar 2025

**Wednesday, May 14, 2025**

𝘼𝙩𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣, 𝙂𝙤𝙧𝙙𝙤𝙣 𝘾𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙚𝙜𝙚 𝙜𝙧𝙖𝙙𝙪𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙙𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙨!

We are inviting you to the 𝗘𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗘𝗻𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗪𝗲𝗯𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗿 entitled 𝗟𝗮𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗲𝗿: 𝗦𝗼𝗳𝘁 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗦𝘂𝗰𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 with 𝗗𝗿. 𝗡𝗼𝗿𝗯𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗼 𝗖𝗮𝘀𝗮𝗯𝗮𝗹, a Leadership Development and Organization Development Consultant.

This will be held on May 21 2025, from 8:30AM – 12:00NN via Zoom and live-streamed on GC – OSWS Facebook Page.

𝗥𝗲𝗴𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝘁𝗵𝗿𝘂 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗸: [https://forms.gle/amTCgHttYoGnU8ZY6](https://forms.gle/amTCgHttYoGnU8ZY6?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExTXZhYzRFZjNQOUxIZk5LbAEeQHQ4wkT4

# LABOR EDUCATION FOR GRADUATING STUDENTS 2025

**Wednesday, May 14, 2025**

𝘼𝙩𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣, 𝙂𝙤𝙧𝙙𝙤𝙣 𝘾𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙚𝙜𝙚 𝙜𝙧𝙖𝙙𝙪𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙙𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙨!

Do not forget to register for the upcoming 𝗟𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿 𝗘𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗚𝗿𝗮𝗱𝘂𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗦𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 on 𝗠𝗮𝘆 𝟮𝟴, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 𝗮𝘁 𝗦𝗠𝗫 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿! Participants will be divided into two sessions: 𝗔𝗠 𝗦𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗖𝗕𝗔 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗖𝗛𝗧𝗠); 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗣𝗠 𝗦𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗖𝗖𝗦, 𝗖𝗘𝗔𝗦 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗖𝗔𝗛𝗦). See you there!

Register thru this link: [https://forms.gle/EEJpb6MLcSh8gCMT6](https://forms.gle/EEJpb6MLcSh8gCMT6?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExTXZhYzRFZjNQOUxIZk5LbAEeJJVnC72gqxKDV6wLXtF4HJt8HT6o7Fb4MFROnwTNuRkT9hN-UpLqCOVKAsk_aem_ZZzrqgnKF7jFhPEe5s1zjw)

Or just scan the QR code below!

Registration is open until May 15, 2025, 3:00 PM.

Don’t forget to like and follow our GC OSWS – CEJPSU Facebook page for more updates!

**Tuesday, May 13, 2025**

Online Enlistment for Enrolment

June 2 – 13, 2025

**Wednesday, June 7, 2023**

Thank you!

**Judging**

The photo contest shall be judged according to the following criteria:

* **Content and   Relevance to the Theme –  30%**

* **Artistic Value and Communication Potential of the Image –  30%**

* **Popularity –  10%**
  + This will be determined by the number of positive reacts (like, love, wow) on Facebooks

* For the popularity criteria, Gordon College will post in its social media page the entries from FEBRUARY 11 – FEBRUARY 12, 2024. The decision of the judges is final and irrevocable.

𝙀𝙮𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚, 𝙂𝘾𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙨!

Here is your gentle reminder that the implementation of proper wearing of school uniform is 𝗲𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗼𝗻 𝗦𝗲𝗽𝘁𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝟭𝟴, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟯.

In case your uniform is not yet available, you must present the 𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗥𝗲𝗰𝗲𝗶𝗽𝘁 issued by the establishment where their uniform was ordered. Further, the official receipts will only be honored only until 𝗦𝗲𝗽𝘁𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝟮𝟵, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟯.

Working students can accomplish their Uniform Exemption pass at  
𝗥𝗠 𝟭𝟬𝟲 – the office of our Student Discipline Coordinator.

---
label: announcement implementation-of-the-proper-wearing-of-school-uniform

# CHED Rapid Survey for Profiling of Students in HEIs

**Friday, September 29, 2023**

This has reference to the request of the CHED of Planning, Research and Knowledge Management (OPRKM) to disseminate its conduct of rapid survey to determine the profile of students currently enrolled in Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) in order for the Commission to develop appropriate initiatives, interventions, and policies needed to ensure that the students will have a successful educational journey.  
  
Relative to this, all HEIs in the Region are enjoined to participate in the said survey thru the
label: announcement ched-rapid-survey-for-profiling-of-students-in-heis

content:

Relative to this, all HEIs in the Region are enjoined to participate in the said survey thru the following links:

* Survey Link for LUCs: [bit.ly/chedsesLUC](http://bit.ly/chedsesLUC)

The portal will be open from September 27 to 30 of 2023.  
  
Rest assured that the commission adheres to the Republic Act 10173 or the Data Privacy Act of 2012 and that no personal information of the students shall be shared with other parties. Data collected will be analyzed for the purpose stated above.  
  
If you have any questions or concerns, please contact Ms. Raphaella Navarrosa through rnavarrosa@c

# Gordon College FUNdation RUN 2025

**Sunday, February 16, 2025**

Gordon College FUNdation RUN 2025 ✨🏃🏻

In line with the celebration of the 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝟮𝟲𝘁𝗵 𝗙𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗔𝗻𝗻𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗮𝗿𝘆, join us on February 23, 2025 for the annual 𝗙𝗨𝗡𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗥𝘂𝗻.

𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗙𝗮𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘆 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗚𝗖𝗙𝗔𝘀), in partnership with the 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗔𝗹𝘂𝗺𝗻𝗶 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗜𝗻𝗰. (𝗚𝗖𝗔𝗔𝗜), brings together faculty, admin personnel, students, and alumni. Wear your running shoes and let’s run as one community. See you there! 🙌🏻

For more information, please visit the 𝗚𝗖𝗙𝗔𝘀 𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗲 at 𝗥𝗠𝟯𝟭𝟮, 𝟯𝗿𝗱 𝗙𝗹𝗼𝗼𝗿, 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲, message us

**Monday, April 21, 2025**

Ａｔｔｅｎｔｉｏｎ ！！！

𝗢𝗦𝗪𝗦 𝗖𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗥𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗡𝗼𝗻-𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗱𝘂𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗦𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀:

Students must be 𝗰𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗼𝗳 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝗽𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝗰𝗶𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗸 from the Student Discipline Unit.

**Monday, February 17, 2025**

In celebration of the Gordon College 26th Founding Anniversary, the RDCES-Community Extension Services Unit and the College of Business and Accountancy invites all job seekers to an exciting Job Fair 2025 on February 26, 2025, at the Gordon College Function Hall, starting at 9 AM onwards. This is your chance to connect with top employers, explore job opportunities, and take the next step in your career journey!

This event is made possible through the support of our valued partners: GC Alumni Services Unit, GC Faculty Association,

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/mental-health-break/]
# Mental Health Break

**Monday, October 23, 2023**

In line with our continued commitment to promoting the well-being of our school community, please be informed that October 31, 2023, is a Mental Health break for our students and faculty members.

There will be no synchronous and face-to-face classes for students and teaching personnel including Related Learning Experiences duties. This is to provided venue for our teachers and students to have a break from academics and school tasks. Moreover, this will help in strengthening and taking care of their mental health.

Please be reminded that no activities, deadlines, or submissions should be given to the students during the academic break.

Regular classes will resume on November 3, 2023.

---
label: announcement mental-health-break

# Payment Cut-Off of Finance Services Unit

**Saturday, January 20, 2024**

𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧’𝗦 𝗨𝗣, 𝗚𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗡𝗦?!

 𝗕𝗔𝗡𝗗𝗦 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟰!

The registration period will be open from January 20 to February 2. The elimination round will be held on February 11, 2024 and only 10 slots will be only be alloted for interested participants.

𝗠𝗘𝗖𝗛𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗖𝗦 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗚𝗨𝗜𝗗𝗘𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗘𝗦:  
1. The competition is open to all Gordon College students, personnel, and alumni.  
2. Students from other schools may join the competition, provided that 𝘮𝘰𝘴𝘵 𝘮𝘦𝘮𝘣𝘦𝘳𝘴 are currently enrolled at Gordon College.  
3. Members should bring school ID, photocopy of school ID, or registration form and 2×2 pictures.  
4. Each band should compose a maximum of 5-7 membe
label: announcement battle-of-the-bands

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/battle-of-the-bands/]
hool ID, or registration form and 2×2 pictures.  
4. Each band should compose a maximum of 5-7 members.  
5. Each band should prepare one OPM song and one foreign song for their performance (original composition is allowed).  
6. Songs should not contain foul words.  
7. Bring your own instruments and equipment except the drum set which will be provided by the event organizers.  
8. During the Elimination:  
• One OPM song and one foreign song will be played by each band  
• NO metal and hard-core genre  
• NO foul words  
• 15 minutes will be given for each band for performance including set

• Late on the call time of the event will mean disqualification from the contest  
9. Top five bands will be qualified to enter the final showdown which will be held on February 22, 2024, at Gordon College Campus Grounds.

𝗖𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗜𝗔 𝗙𝗢𝗥 𝗝𝗨𝗗𝗚𝗜𝗡𝗚 (𝗘𝗹𝗶𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻):

# ONSITE PRC SERVICE CARAVAN

**Wednesday, January 31, 2024**

**Ａ Ｔ Ｔ Ｅ Ｎ Ｔ Ｉ Ｏ Ｎ Ｐ Ｌ Ｅ Ａ Ｓ Ｅ !**

We are thrilled to announce that in the upcoming 25th Founding Anniversary of our institution, 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝗦𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗪𝗲𝗹𝗳𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗰𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗔𝗹𝘂𝗺𝗻𝗶 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗜𝗻𝗰., 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗥𝗲𝗴𝘂𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 – there will be a 𝙤𝙣𝙚-𝙙𝙖𝙮 𝙋𝙍𝘾 𝙎𝙚𝙧𝙫𝙞𝙘𝙚𝙨 𝘾𝙖𝙧𝙖𝙫𝙖𝙣 on 𝗙𝗲𝗯𝗿𝘂𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝟮𝟭, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟰.

𝗦𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗰𝗲𝘀 𝗮𝘃𝗮𝗶𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲:

Application for Licensure Examination

Renewal and Duplication of Professional Identification Card

Issuance of Certifications and Authentication PRC Documents

Petition

# MR. & MS. GORDON COLLEGE 2024

**Friday, January 26, 2024**

Who will emerge victorious and wear the coveted crown?

In partnership with 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀, we present to you, the candidates vying for the highly anticipated title of 𝗠𝗿. 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗠𝘀. 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟰 in their stunning and regal headshot!

𝗩𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘄! The candidates with the highest number of engagement (heart reacts and share) will win the 𝗠𝗿. & 𝗠𝘀. 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 award during the coronation night on Feb. 21, 2024.

𝗩𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝘀 𝘂𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗹 𝗙𝗲𝗯 𝟮𝟬, 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟰, 𝟭𝗣𝗠.

# GC-SSC PISO PRINT PROJECT

**Thursday, October 17, 2024**

Sa Piso Print, sulit at abot-kaya ang bawat pahina! 𝗣𝗿𝗶𝗻𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗽𝗵𝗼𝘁𝗼𝗰𝗼𝗽𝘆 𝗒𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝖽𝗈𝖼𝗎𝗆𝖾𝗇𝗍𝗌 𝖿𝗈𝗋 𝗈𝗇𝗅𝗒 1 𝗉𝖾𝗌𝗈 𝗉𝖾𝗋 𝗉𝖺𝗀𝖾—𝗉𝖾𝗋𝖿𝖾𝖼𝗍 𝖿𝗈𝗋 𝗌𝗍𝗎𝖽𝖾𝗇𝗍𝗌 𝗐𝗁𝗈 𝗇𝖾𝖾𝖽 𝗊𝗎𝖺𝗅𝗂𝗍𝗒 𝗉𝗋𝗂𝗇𝗍𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝖺𝗍 𝖺 𝖻𝗎𝖽𝗀𝖾𝗍-𝖿𝗋𝗂𝖾𝗇𝖽𝗅𝗒 𝗉𝗋𝗂𝖼𝖾.

𝙍𝙀𝙈𝙄𝙉𝘿𝙀𝙍: 𝙋𝙍𝙄𝙉𝙏𝙄𝙉𝙂 𝙒𝙄𝙇𝙇 𝙊𝙉𝙇𝙔 𝘽𝙀 𝘼𝙑𝘼𝙄𝙇𝘼𝘽𝙇𝙀 𝙄𝙉 𝘽𝙇𝘼𝘾𝙆 𝘼𝙉𝘿 𝙒𝙃𝙄𝙏𝙀, 𝘼𝙉𝘿 𝘼4 𝙎𝙄𝙕𝙀 𝙐𝙉𝙏𝙄𝙇 𝙁𝙐𝙍𝙏𝙃𝙀𝙍 𝙉𝙊𝙏𝙄𝘾𝙀.

This initiative reflects our genuine care for every GCian! Don’t miss the chance to enjoy this exclusive printing service from GC-SSC.

Basta GCian, only the best for you—GC ‘yan eh

4.3 The image file should be less than 2MB to avoid uploading errors

o PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate

o Senior High School ID or Any Valid ID Card

– Click “Submit” to finalize the uploading.

# Project Upskill: DepEd Senior High School Teachers

**Tuesday, November 12, 2024**

Gordon College CCS Project Upskill: DepEd SHS Teachers

In line with the preparation for the 𝟭𝟭𝘁𝗵 𝗣𝗵𝗼𝗲𝗻𝗶𝘅 𝗢𝗹𝘆𝗺𝗽𝗶𝗮𝗱 on February, 2025, Gordon College – College of Computer Studies (GC-CCS) Community Extension Services Unit (CESU) will be launching 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗨𝗽𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹: 𝗗𝗲𝗽𝗘𝗱 𝗦𝗛𝗦 𝗧𝗲𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗿𝘀, an upskilling program for DepEd Senior High School Teachers, with the theme “Empowering Educators for the Digital Age: Bridging the Gap with Technology” on November 15 and 22, 2024 from 8:00AM to 5:00PM at Gordon College, Olongapo
label: announcement project-upskill-deped-senior-high-school-teachers

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/project-upskill-deped-senior-high-school-teachers/]
e Gap with Technology” on November 15 and 22, 2024 from 8:00AM to 5:00PM at Gordon College, Olongapo City.

The training program will focus on essential technology skills across five key areas:

1. Capture the Flag (Cybersecurity): Introducing teachers to cybersecurity fundamentals through the CTF game.  
2. Game Design: Covering basic principles of game development, from concept creation to design and implementation.  
3. Animation: Teaching the core techniques of animation and how to apply them using industry-standard tools.  
4. Video Editing: Providing instruction on video production and e
label: announcement project-upskill-deped-senior-high-school-teachers

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/project-upskill-deped-senior-high-school-teachers/]
m using industry-standard tools.  
4. Video Editing: Providing instruction on video production and editing for multimedia storytelling.  
5. IoT (Internet of Things)/ Ideation: Exploring the potential of IoT in education and developing innovative solutions involving interconnected devices.

Participants may register online using the link:  
[https://bit.ly/GCCCSProjectUpskill](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fbit.ly%2FGCCCSProjectUpskill%3Ffbclid%3DIwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR0DTKAT2UuYX3bYqrHUA03AVE_XE4nwlTshbuon_ZbR9aLVBAsXNmj_hCs_aem_pI4koYBDJfcQElqFMHoIGA&h=AT3Vx9LNN7uXLaRRh8VgwP-sbAvJ

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions/]
# GUIDELINES TO SAFEGUARD THE STUDENTS’ HEALTH AND WELL-BEING DURING EXTREME HEAT CONDITIONS

**Tuesday, April 23, 2024**

𝙀𝙮𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚, 𝙂𝘾𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙨!

Thru a memondarum released by the Office of the College President, the following guidelines shall be observed to safeguard the health and welfare of the students:

1. Students with respiratory illnesses or heat-sensitive health conditions such as hypertension, and those with disabilities and special learning needs, may be excused from in-person classes. Adequate resources and support should be provided to ensure their continued learning.

2. The conduct
label: announcement guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions/]
dequate resources and support should be provided to ensure their continued learning.

2. The conduct of activities that expose students to direct sunlight or require excessive physical exertion during extreme heat conditions is not permitted.

3. On days when the heat index is forecasted to reach 42°c (dangerous) in Olongapo City, colleges may transition to online learning to prioritize the health and welfare of both students and faculty except for related learning experience, practicum, on-the-job training, or internships. Students coming from areas with a heat index forecasted to reach 42°c
label: announcement guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions/]
job training, or internships. Students coming from areas with a heat index forecasted to reach 42°c may request their instructors to excuse them from their classes.

4. Faculty, staff, and students are encouraged to carry water during class and work hours to stay hydrated.

5. For any health-related concerns, kindly proceed to the College Clinic (RM 120).

6. The requirement for students, faculty, and staff to wear uniforms is temporarily relaxed until further notice, however, the dress code should still be followed.

7. The library and function hall are designated as student lounges during th
label: announcement guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions/]
uld still be followed.

7. The library and function hall are designated as student lounges during the hot season except when there are previously scheduled activities in these areas.

---
label: announcement guidelines-to-safeguard-the-students-health-and-well-being-during-extreme-heat-conditions

# STUDENTS’ PERCEPTION

**Thursday, April 25, 2024**

𝗔𝗧𝗧𝗘𝗡𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡, 𝗚𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗡𝗦!

This past few weeks, Olongapo City along with its nearby provinces are currently experiencing a severe heat index ranging from 40 – 42 Degrees Celsius and it is also expected to reach a higher level in the following weeks according to the Philippine Atmospheric Geophysical and Astronomical Services (PAG-ASA).

Through careful consideration of the dangers brought by the dangerous heat index we are currently experiencing, the Gordon College – Supreme Student Council seek permission last April 22, 2024, to the Office of the
label: announcement students-perception

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/students-perception/]
e Gordon College – Supreme Student Council seek permission last April 22, 2024, to the Office of the College President to conduct an online survey to gather the student body’s consensus regarding the temporary shifting of onsite classes to online class to limit the possible dangers that both students and faculty members might encounter.

In line with this matter, we, the Gordon College – Supreme Student Council is asking for every student’s cooperation to answer a short survey regarding the students’ perception on shifting onsite classes to online class.

Rest assured that the results will be
label: announcement students-perception

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/students-perception/]
ents’ perception on shifting onsite classes to online class.

Rest assured that the results will be forecasted with utmost transparency and personal information will not be shared to ensure confidentiality of the students.

You may access the survey thru the link provided below. The link will only be accepting responses until 𝐀𝐏𝐑𝐈𝐋 𝟐𝟕, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒 until 𝟏𝟐 𝐧𝐧.

August 5 – 16, 2024

Step 1

Log-on to the student portal ([https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/ccs/student](https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/ccs/student?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR3KSzEygqwv8s_M3_yUMJL0ooECnitYkwTBevsCr520xZAdSaF60njLqYU_aem_IKPRzhR4H4Jk5q0HpWSm7w))

To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.

To request for adding of classes, under Add
label: announcement online-adding-dropping-of-classes

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/online-adding-dropping-of-classes/]
ses, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.

To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.

Step 3

Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.

Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be approved by the college registrar. Regularly vi
